title: My Time Is Allowed to Be Enough
subtitle: A three-minute affirmation for the woman who feels she is always behind
excerpt: A three-minute affirmation for the woman who feels she is always behind. The affirmation does not promise more hours. It changes the contract with the hours that are already there.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The enough-time affirmation shape is a staple of writing on time scarcity.

content.text: A three-minute affirmation for the woman who feels she is always behind. The hours are what they are. The affirmation changes the contract with them.
New to affirmation as a practice? Read
Many of us live inside a story that says we are behind. Behind on the work, behind on the house, behind on the children, behind on the body. The story runs in the background and shapes the day.
The affirmation interrupts the story. It says the time you have is allowed to be the time you have. The catch-up project is closed.
My time is allowed to be enough. The catch-up project is closed.
Say it three times in the morning, hand on the heart. Say it slowly. The third time can be the slowest.
When the behind-feeling comes back during the day, say it once again, in your head, while you keep doing whatever you are doing.
Original to homemade.education. The enough-time affirmation shape is a common one. It appears in many books on time and on calm.
